@rizaardiyanto Three things that can consume usage in the background

- automations
- memory
- suggested prompts

To celebrate the launch of GPT-5.6 Sol, we will reset the rate limits again (twice) across ChatGPT Work and Codex over the next 24 hours.

We want you to have the time to truly try ambitious tasks and get the hang of it. Happy exploring!
@Youssofal_ I would like to point out that you are on `ultra`!

I recommend using GPT-5.6 Sol Medium as your daily driver, and for really hard problems to switch to Extra High. 

If you want the absolute best and are not afraid to burn usage fast, then Ultra is a beast.
There is often a 5-10x difference in token spend between Medium and Ultra depending on how hard the task is.

Or a generational run. We will continue to make tweaks and we have a TON coming for developers. Meanwhile traffic yesterday sat at ~2X previous peak, team is scrambling to keep everything up and reliable.

- Full access mode is enabled and codex is run without sandboxing protections, including without auto review being

uncertainties: The claim may be a joke or metaphor rather than evidence of a reset.
It does not identify whether any actual reset occurred.
Ambiguous, playful, conditional, or interrogative reset wording requires review before it can affect the forecast.
evidence_quotes: I actually stole their reset button.
2077626528020697173.relevance_reason: Mentions a reset button, but the statement appears playful or figurative and does not clearly confirm a real reset event.

uncertainties: The statement that ChatGPT and Codex were merged is presented casually and is not independently verifiable from the post.
The proposed next move is rhetorical/speculative rather than a clear commitment.
evidence_quotes: Now that we merged ChatGPT and Codex, what should we merge next?
What's the double or nothing move.
2077627035418239230.relevance_reason: The post discusses merging ChatGPT and Codex and asks for ideas about a future merger, but provides no evidence of a reset, usage incident, capacity concern, or reset-related policy change.

uncertainties: The post does not specify whether the removal of the 5-hour limit is an official permanent policy change or a temporary test.
The questions about managing weekly usage are exploratory and do not confirm a reset or a future change.
evidence_quotes: We've had no 5h limit in Codex plus and pro for a few days.
If we were to make this different, what should it look like in an ideal world?
2077632589498913087.relevance_reason: The post explicitly discusses the absence of the 5-hour Codex usage limit and solicits feedback on changing the usage model.
